agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable — kiro appends each AssistantMessage as one whole JSONL line after generation; streaming is TUI-render only, so no partial-flush trace reaches the file the daemon tails
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable — kiro appends each AssistantMessage as one whole JSONL line after generation; the streamed reply reaches the on-disk transcript all-at-once, so the no-flicker invariant this scenario pins has no kiro trace to observe
driver_capability: ready

Route: frozen. Same shape as the pi and codex cells for this row: the agent streams to the terminal, but the transcript the daemon tails carries only whole, atomically-appended assistant lines — there is no non-terminal streaming-chunk family for the parser to hold `working` across.

## What the scenario pins

`streaming-partial-writes` (primitive `streaming_output`): the assistant message arrives in multiple partial flushes before the turn-end signal, and the irrlicht invariant is that those partial flushes MUST NOT trigger a premature `→ ready` (no `ready → working → ready` flicker before end-of-turn). The canonical claudecode mechanism is `resolveAssistantStreaming` (parser.go:288) downgrading non-terminal `stop_reason` lines to `assistant_streaming`, excluded from `IsAgentDone()` — Bug-D streaming-flicker family, issue #102. The `streaming_window` phase pins the no-`→ready` invariant across the flush window.

## agent_supports: yes

kiro-cli streams assistant output incrementally: the live 2.5.1 smoke recorded a per-turn TUI completion marker (`▸ Credits: 0.03 • Time: 1s`) and the reply renders token-by-token in the TUI (FINDINGS.md §6). So the agent performs streaming output — same as pi (agent=yes).

## daemon_capability: incapable

The live 2.5.1 smoke is decisive: kiro appends the COMPLETE `AssistantMessage` as ONE whole JSONL line AFTER generation finishes — turn 1's `.jsonl` held exactly `Prompt + AssistantMessage` immediately after the reply, with no intermediate partial-assistant lines (FINDINGS.md §5). Streaming happens only in the TUI render, never in the transcript file the daemon tails.

The kiro parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/parser.go:45-83`) only ever sees whole atomic lines: an `AssistantMessage` with `toolUse` blocks → `assistant` (mid-turn, sustains `working`); a text-only `AssistantMessage` → `turn_done` (the working→ready settle). There is NO `assistant_streaming` / non-terminal `stop_reason` concept — the kiro JSONL vocabulary is `Prompt`, `AssistantMessage`, `ToolResults`, `Clear` (FINDINGS.md §4), with no partial-flush family. So the specific `resolveAssistantStreaming` flicker invariant this scenario pins has no kiro analogue: the completed reply lands all-at-once, the daemon emits `ready → working → ready` essentially instantaneously, and there are no intermediate partial flushes for the streaming_window to assert over.

The partial-write tolerance the scenario name evokes — a TORN trailing line (assistant bytes flushed mid-line, no `\n`) — is the SHARED tailer's job (`core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go` `errPartialAtEOF`: stop without advancing past the partial bytes, re-read next tick), already locked down by synthetic unit tests (`core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go` `TestTailAndProcess_PartialTrailingLine`, `TestReadLineCapped_PartialAtEOF`). It is parser-agnostic and a byte-timing artifact a live driver cannot deterministically force, so a kiro recording adds no fidelity there.

## driver_capability: ready

The recipe needs only `send` / `wait_turn` / `sleep`, all listed in the kiro driver's `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"` (`replaydata/agents/kiro-cli/driver-interactive.sh`). No primitive gap.

## Conclusion

Cell frozen at `daemon=incapable`. The recipe + spec are retained as the DOCUMENTED TARGET (the state-stability arc a recording would show, and the no-flicker ideal) should kiro ever switch to incremental rollout writes — but as of 2.5.1 the transcript records whole assistant lines atomically, so the streaming-flicker invariant is unobservable for kiro and no live recording is taken.
caveats: agent_supports is yes (kiro streams to the TUI), but daemon_capability is incapable: the live 2.5.1 smoke (FINDINGS.md §5) proved each AssistantMessage lands as ONE whole JSONL line after generation — incremental streaming is TUI-render only and never reaches the JSONL the daemon tails, so the streaming-window no-flicker invariant has no on-disk trace to assert over.

The torn/partial-trailing-line robustness the scenario name evokes is a shared-tailer property (tailer.go errPartialAtEOF) covered by synthetic unit tests (large_line_test.go), not a kiro-recordable behavior; a live CLI cannot be forced to emit a mid-line flush on demand (that concern lives in the separate oversized-transcript-line cell).

JSONL carries no model/token/cost (FINDINGS.md §4) — sidecar <uuid>.json only — so any kiro recording observes model unknown, cost_nonzero false, tokens_nonzero false. Frozen here, so no recording is taken.

expected.jsonl is retained as the documented target (one contiguous working→ready arc, no premature ready, no waiting) should kiro switch to incremental rollout writes; today the turn lands all-at-once so there is no partial-flush window to validate.

sources.note: §5: kiro appends the COMPLETE AssistantMessage as one whole JSONL line after generation; turn 1's .jsonl had exactly Prompt+AssistantMessage immediately after the reply — no intermediate partial-assistant lines. §6: streaming is TUI-render only (per-turn ▸ Credits marker). §4: event vocabulary is Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear with no model/usage/cost and no streaming-chunk family.

Lines 45-83: AssistantMessage with toolUse → assistant (mid-turn, sustains working); text-only AssistantMessage → turn_done. No assistant_streaming / non-terminal stop_reason concept — the parser only ever sees whole atomic lines, so there is no partial-flush family for a streaming_window to hold working across.
Source is FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser} — append-only JSONL the daemon tails; streamed partial content never reaches it.
errPartialAtEOF path: on a torn trailing line the tailer stops without advancing the offset and re-reads next tick — the actual partial-write robustness mechanism, parser-agnostic, not kiro-specific.

TestTailAndProcess_PartialTrailingLine / TestReadLineCapped_PartialAtEOF: synthetic unit tests that already lock down partial-line tolerance and re-read-once-flushed settling, independent of any adapter.

Calibration: pi is the same shape (agent=yes, daemon=incapable) — pi writes completed messages atomically with no partial-flush trace; streaming is terminal-UI only. kiro matches this exactly per the live smoke.
